title: pseudoprosopis euryphylla subsp. puguensis
intro: Pseudoprosopis euryphylla subsp. puguensis, also known as Pugu, is a plant species native to Tanzania, classified as a shrub or small tree in the Fabaceae family. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, attributed to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Pugu are said to include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant. Pugu can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves are sometimes dried and crushed for use in traditional remedies.
